Protocol XYZ-001: Phase III Randomized Controlled Trial

A Multicenter, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase III Study to Evaluate
the Efficacy and Safety of Compound BKN-4521 in Patients with Moderate to
Severe Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD)

1. STUDY DESIGN

This is a Phase III, multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled,
parallel-group study designed to evaluate the efficacy and safety of BKN-4521
administered orally once daily for 52 weeks in patients with moderate to severe
COPD as defined by GOLD criteria (FEV1 30-79% predicted).

Approximately 1,200 patients will be randomized in a 1:1 ratio to receive either
BKN-4521 200mg or matching placebo. Randomization will be stratified by baseline
FEV1 severity (moderate vs. severe) and smoking status (current vs. former).

2. PRIMARY ENDPOINTS

The co-primary efficacy endpoints are:
- Change from baseline in pre-bronchodilator FEV1 at Week 24
- Rate of moderate or severe COPD exacerbations over 52 weeks

3. KEY SECONDARY ENDPOINTS

- Change from baseline in St. George's Respiratory Questionnaire (SGRQ) total
  score at Week 24
- Time to first moderate or severe COPD exacerbation
- Change from baseline in 6-minute walk distance (6MWD) at Week 24
- Proportion of SGRQ responders (decrease of >= 4 units) at Week 24

4. INCLUSION CRITERIA

Patients must meet ALL of the following criteria:
- Age >= 40 years at screening
- Current or former smoker with >= 10 pack-year history
- Documented diagnosis of COPD (GOLD criteria) for >= 12 months
- Post-bronchodilator FEV1/FVC ratio < 0.70
- Post-bronchodilator FEV1 >= 30% and < 80% of predicted normal
- >= 1 documented COPD exacerbation requiring systemic corticosteroids or
  antibiotics or hospitalization in the 12 months prior to screening

5. SAMPLE SIZE JUSTIFICATION

The sample size of 600 patients per arm was determined based on the following
assumptions: a clinically meaningful difference in FEV1 of 100 mL between
groups, standard deviation of 250 mL, two-sided significance level of 0.025
(adjusted for co-primary endpoints), 90% power, and an anticipated dropout
rate of 20% over 52 weeks. The exacerbation rate endpoint assumes a baseline
rate of 1.2 events per year, a 25% rate reduction, and uses a negative
binomial regression model with an over-dispersion parameter of 0.5.

6. SAFETY MONITORING

An independent Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) will review unblinded
safety data at pre-specified intervals. Stopping rules are based on the
Lan-DeMets alpha-spending function with O'Brien-Fleming boundaries for
efficacy and Haybittle-Peto rule for safety.

7. STATISTICAL ANALYSIS

The primary analysis will use the modified intent-to-treat (mITT) population.
Missing FEV1 data will be handled using a mixed model for repeated measures
(MMRM) approach. The exacerbation rate will be analyzed using a negative
binomial regression model with treatment, baseline FEV1, smoking status,
and region as covariates.
